meaning: adj.神经过敏的,战战兢兢的,抖动着
examples: All this talk of job losses was making him jittery.
International investors have become jittery about the country's economy.
It also wants to act before too many jittery suppliers start refusing to do business with Daiei.
Her evidence has certainly left some in Cardiff Bay feeling "pretty jittery" a reliable source tells me.
